dashboard.seoDescription: Guide to using the JLPT N1-N5 exam grading system. How to enter answers, read results, and adjust weights.
dashboard.title: How to Use the Grading System
dashboard.description: This app helps you estimate your JLPT mock exam score by mondai, track section scores, and quickly check pass conditions based on total score and minimum section thresholds.
dashboard.warningDescription: These score estimates are for reference only, as the JLPT organizers do not publicly disclose their official scoring method.
dashboard.quickFlowStep1: 1. Open the JLPT Grading page and select level N1 - N5.
dashboard.quickFlowStep2: 2. Enter the number of correct answers for each mondai.
dashboard.quickFlowStep3: 3. Adjust weights if you want to simulate a specific exam.
dashboard.quickFlowStep4: 4. Read total score, section scores, and pass/fail result.
dashboard.scoringFormula: Section score = (weighted score earned / max weighted score) x section max score.
dashboard.scoringTotal: Total score = sum of section scores, max 180 points.
dashboard.scoringCondition: A pass requires meeting both the total score threshold and all section minimums.
dashboard.notesFluctuation: The number of questions per mondai may vary slightly between actual exams.
dashboard.notesWeight: Higher weights are for questions with more context, length, or complexity.
dashboard.notesUsage: Use results to adjust your study strategy, not as a replacement for official JLPT scores.
dashboard.readResultsTitle: How to Read Results After Grading
dashboard.readResultsDescription: First check pass/fail status, then identify which sections need improvement.
dashboard.readResultsFailure: If your total score passes but you still fail, it's usually because you hit the minimum threshold in one section. Focus on that section before raising your total score.
dashboard.readResultsWeight: The system allows weight adjustment to simulate exams at different difficulty levels. You can quickly reset to default weights.
dashboard.resultNoteDescription: Note: These score estimates are for reference only, as the JLPT organizers do not publicly disclose their official scoring method.

grading.seoDescription: Estimate your JLPT exam score by mondai. Select level N1-N5, enter correct answers, customize weights, and check pass conditions.
grading.title: Estimate Score by Mondai
grading.description: Select a level, enter correct answers, and customize weights to simulate an exam. Results show reference scores and minimum score conditions.
grading.inputTableDescription: Each mondai allows adjusting total questions within ±1 to match actual exams. You can quickly reset weights to defaults.
grading.formulaText: section score = (total weighted score earned / total max weighted score) x section max score.
grading.resultsDescription: Total score and pass conditions are recalculated in real-time after each data change.
grading.validationWarning: Some inputs are invalid. Current results have been calculated with safe boundary values to avoid inaccuracy.
grading.sectionFailTitle: Does not meet minimum score requirement

settings.seoDescription: Customize the JLPT grading app interface.
settings.title: Settings
settings.interfaceDescription: Customize the JLPT grading app interface.
settings.themeDescription: Choose light, dark, or system theme.
settings.languageDescription: Choose the display language for the app.
